author: nflaig
content:
I have a PR https://github.com/ChainSafe/lodestar/pull/9336 that works with your changes

but I am curious Michael, what are the spec tests that Lighthouse is failing? I am not sure which ones should catch this

I am not sure if this is a gap in the tests or just the way we initialize in lodestar, we only need the anchor state, we don't use the block at all, not even during checkpoint sync

author: nflaig
content:
I build a custom image for now to make testing easier
```yaml
ethereum_genesis_generator_params:
  image: nflaig/ethereum-genesis-generator:eth-beacon-genesis-v0.0.3
```

author: sproul
content: yeah it’s a bit quirky, but it’s the fork choice tests that fail when we have the wrong genesis block. We do a thing for those tests where we construct a full BeaconChain and when the genesis block calc is wrong that beacon chain fails to start up because it can’t find the head block

author: potuz
content: Ah no sir, you want to drag me into a genesis argument?

author: nflaig
content: was thinking it should be fork choice tests but we only use the `anchor_state` so we don't run into this issue at all, the root mismatch just happens because I added this check here https://github.com/ChainSafe/lodestar/pull/7863 but I believe this was for some testnet to sanity check the genesis config

author: nflaig
content: <@654267572107083777> I don't think Nimbus cares at all about the changes from Michael, you don't even have the root check we do

author: nflaig
content: yes, it does not

author: tersec
content: well last time around when some interop changes broke things, I made it it look at both the most recent bid and latest block hash

author: tersec
content: because one was spec and the other was the genesis generator

author: tersec
content: so now it just picks the nonzero one
